reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P network by crafting malicious cells with non-zero coefficients in the upper half [FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB, FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB). The recover_cells function (recovery.c:201) performs coset_ifft to recover the full polynomial but fails to validate the post-condition deg(poly) < FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB. This violates the erasure coding assumption and can cause compute_fk20_cell_proofs to generate invalid proofs, potentially leading to availability failures or network splits. All three gates passed: (1) function has active callers in production code and bindings, (2) cells input is untrusted P2P data per eip7594.c:218 comment, (3) PeerDAS/EIP-7594 is in-scope per contest rules.
